description: Register FR-20260312-009 (Founder Decision P-20260312-006), create proposal files P-20260312-005 and P-20260312-006, and insert trace_event (operational_governance_rule_enacted). CRC-04 closed.

# Founder Decision

The Founder approves Proposal P-20260312-006 "Collector Governance Framework".

This decision establishes operational governance rules for Collector concurrency,
trace_event generation, and Collector lifecycle management within the AI Trading OS.

## Key Points

- Hybrid Collector Model (Primary / Secondary) is formally adopted
- canonical_observation / supplemental_observation trace_event model is adopted
- Collector Lifecycle governance (suspension, demotion, deactivation, disqualification,
  Primary Collector succession) is formally defined
- base_collector_v1 is designated as Primary Collector
- P-20260310-009 (AI Collector Evaluation Framework) remains in effect as the
  entry-stage governance; P-20260312-006 governs concurrent operation thereafter

No constitutional amendment is performed by this decision.

## CRC Status

CRC Deliberation 04 is formally closed by this Founder decision.

## Scope Statement

This proposal establishes operational governance rules for the classification,
update authority, and update triggers of anchor documents in AI Trading OS.

This proposal is not a constitutional amendment. Anchor documents are not
institutional records. This proposal clarifies their operational position.

## Documentation Layers

### Layer A — Institutional Records (Source of Truth)

- constitution.md
- proposals/*
- founder_records/*
- research.trace_event

### Layer B — Institutional State Snapshot

- docs/anchors/AI_TRADING_OS_MASTER_ANCHOR.md

Update authority: AI proposal -> Librarian approval

### Layer C — AI Operational Context

- CLAUDE.md

Update authority: AI proposal -> Founder approval

## Principles

- Anchors are not Source of Truth
- Anchors should change rarely
- AI agents must not autonomously modify any layer

## Draft Anchor Process

draft anchor -> proposal artifact -> review -> Founder approval
-> formal anchor update or discard record

---
proposal_id: P-20260312-006
title: "Collector Governance Framework"
status: accepted
date: 2026-03-12
revision: 1
author: Proposer (Claude)
role: Proposer
context_references:
  - constitution.md (v1.3)
  - P-20260310-007 (Collector Role Definition)
  - P-20260310-008 (base_collector_v1)
  - P-20260310-009 (AI Collector Evaluation Framework)
  - P-20260310-011 (OpenClaw Provisional Invitation)
  - CRC Deliberation 04
amendment_type: operational_governance_rule
crc_required: false
---

## Scope Statement

This proposal establishes operational governance rules for Collector concurrency,
trace_event generation model, and Collector lifecycle management in AI Trading OS.

This proposal is not a constitutional amendment. Constitution Article 4 role
boundary (observation only, no interpretation) is preserved as the foundation.

## Part I — Collector Concurrency Model (Hybrid)

### Primary Collector

- Generates canonical_observation trace_events
- Designated by Founder after Stage evaluation (P-20260310-009)
- Current designation: base_collector_v1

### Secondary Collector

- Generates supplemental_observation trace_events
- Functions as fallback when Primary is unavailable

### Instrument Scope (supplementary concept)

Optional per-Collector instrument assignment to structurally prevent
observation conflicts.

## Part II — trace_event Generation Model (Model C)

Primary -> event_type: canonical_observation
Secondary -> event_type: supplemental_observation

metadata must include: collector_id, observation_class, instrument

No trace_event schema change required.

## Part III — Collector Lifecycle

| Event | Condition | Authority | trace_event |
|---|---|---|---|
| Temporary Suspension | Quality degradation / technical failure | Librarian | collector_suspended |
| Reactivation | Cause resolved | Librarian | collector_reactivated |
| Demotion (Primary->Secondary) | Better Collector designated / quality drop | Librarian recommendation + Founder approval | collector_demoted |
| Permanent Deactivation | Serious violation / role boundary breach | Librarian recommendation + Founder approval | collector_deactivated |
| Disqualification | Deactivation + no re-approval | Librarian recommendation + Founder approval + Founder Record | collector_deactivated (disqualified: true) |
| Primary Succession | System-critical operational change | Proposal -> Librarian review -> Founder approval | primary_collector_changed |

Disqualification does not delete institutional history. append-only principle applies.

## Part IV — Lifecycle trace_event Types

canonical_observation, supplemental_observation,
collector_suspended, collector_reactivated,
collector_demoted, collector_deactivated,
primary_collector_changed

## Limitations

- P-20260310-009 entry-stage evaluation remains in effect
- This proposal governs concurrent operation, not entry evaluation

---
